action.reasoning: Observation: torchrun rendezvous keeps timing out (TCPStore can't connect). This is a network/hostname issue. Analysis: Better to use torch.multiprocessing spawn directly to avoid torchrun's TCPStore. I can set MASTER_ADDR=127.0.0.1 and MASTER_PORT explicitly with init_method='env://' or use 'tcp://127.0.0.1:port'. Decision: Modify train.py to use mp.spawn with explicit init_method, or use single-node manual init. Let me set MASTER_ADDR/MASTER_PORT env vars and use torchrun --rdzv_backend=static or just use python -m torch.distributed.launch alternatives. Simplest: use mp.spawn with init_method='tcp://127.0.0.1:29500'.
action.command: cd /workspace && timeout 600 python launch.py --epochs 1 --batch_size 4 2>err.log; echo EXIT=$?; tail -100 err.log
